title: Zinia's Confetti Layout
excerpt: Hello everyone! It’s Zinia here and I have another paper-focused design to share with you. This is an extremely easy page to recreate and it’s perfect if you want to use up scraps. My starting point for this layout was…
body:
Hello everyone! It’s Zinia here and I have another paper-focused design to share with you. This is an extremely easy page to recreate and it’s perfect if you want to use up scraps.

My starting point for this layout was a color palette. I realized that many of my pages look similar cause I tend to either use one collection at a time or gravitate towards the same color combinations. That’s why I decided to step outside my comfort zone and work with a very unusual(atleast for my standard) color palette.

I basically mixed some reds, pinks and oranges with green and teals patterns. To achieve that color combination, I pulled papers from various collections by Amy. Although the collections look completely different from one another, the products work together effortlessly.

So after I pulled my pattern papers, I punched a bunch of circles in two different sizes. I arranged those circles on my page pretty much randomly. I only tried to keep them denser in the middle and spread them around as we move to the edges. I call this, the confetti design!

To make my confetti area even more dense, I added a few tiny splatters to fiil any big gaps between my circles. The embellishing part was slightly challenging because of the unusual color palette. I had to focus on pieces that were either monochromatic or didn’t include any extra colors.

I must say that having limited options because of the colors, made it much easier to commit and finish off the page very quickly instead of trying out every single piece of ephemera in my stash.

So this is the page for today, I really hope you enjoyed it and you will pull out your circle punches to create your own take on this confetti design.

Until next time, happy crafting.

- Zinia
